description: Five Parsecs From Home - Game Keyword Definitions

reactions.definition: Determines activation order and how quickly a character responds in combat. Characters activate from highest to lowest Reactions. Used for initiative rolls.

combat_skill.definition: Modifier added to hit rolls in both ranged and brawling combat. Higher values improve accuracy. Standard human baseline is 0.

toughness.definition: Resistance to damage. When hit, roll 1d6 + weapon damage vs Toughness. If roll exceeds Toughness, target becomes a casualty.

savvy.definition: Mental acuity and awareness. Used for tests involving perception, technology, negotiation, and recovering from being pinned.

speed.definition: Base movement distance in inches. Standard movement is Speed value. Dash allows double movement but prevents shooting.

luck.definition: Resource spent to reroll dice or avoid death. Each point allows one reroll per battle. Regenerates at the start of each campaign turn.

assault.definition: Can be fired without penalty while moving at combat speed. Ideal for aggressive tactics and mobile firefights.

auto.definition: Fires multiple shots per action. +1 shot per activation, increasing hit chances but consuming more ammunition.

heavy.definition: Cannot be used after moving at combat speed. Requires setup, but typically offers increased damage or range.

bulky.definition: Cannot be carried by Soulless. Takes up 2 equipment slots instead of 1. Typically heavy or awkward weapons.

pistol.definition: Can be fired while engaged in brawling without penalty. Easy to conceal and quick to draw.

melee.definition: Close combat weapon. Used in brawling attacks. Cannot be used for ranged attacks.

snap_shot.definition: Can fire as a reaction during enemy movement within line of sight. Useful for area denial.

area.definition: Affects all figures within the blast radius. Roll to hit for each target in the area. Ignores cover.

clumsy.definition: Awkward to use. -1 to hit rolls with this weapon.

critical.definition: On a natural 6 to hit, add +1 damage. Devastating against high-Toughness targets.

elegant.definition: Precision weapon. +1 to hit rolls. Often associated with refined or ancient weapons.

focused.definition: If the shooter does not move, gains +1 to hit. Rewards careful aim over mobility.

impact.definition: +1 damage at point blank range. Devastating in close quarters.

piercing.definition: Ignores armor saves. Effective against heavily armored targets.

stun.definition: On hit, target must pass Savvy test or become Stunned. Does not deal damage.

terrifying.definition: Enemies within sight must pass morale test or become Pinned. Powerful psychological effect.

single_use.definition: Can only be used once per battle. After firing, the item is expended.

stunned.definition: Cannot activate this round. If already activated, ends turn immediately. Can still defend in brawling. Clears at end of round.

pinned.definition: Cannot move or shoot. Must pass Savvy test at start of activation to recover. Can still defend in brawling.

out_of_action.definition: Removed from battle. Roll on post-battle casualty table to determine if permanently killed, injured, or recovered.

brawling.definition: Close combat between adjacent figures. Both sides roll 1d6 + Combat Skill. Highest roll wins. Loser becomes casualty or stunned.

cover.definition: Partial obstruction between shooter and target. Provides +1 to target's Toughness against ranged attacks. Does not help in brawling.

heavy_cover.definition: Substantial protection like walls or thick barriers. +2 to target's Toughness against ranged attacks.

difficult_ground.definition: Rough terrain that slows movement. Costs double movement to traverse. Examples: rubble, mud, dense vegetation.

impassable.definition: Cannot be moved through. Must go around. Examples: solid walls, deep water, lava.

elevated.definition: Higher ground position. Provides +1 to ranged attacks and improved line of sight. Examples: rooftops, hills.

linear_obstacle.definition: Low barrier like walls or fences. Provides cover when behind. Costs 2 inches of movement to cross.

screen.definition: Personal energy shield. Provides 5+ armor save. Can be worn with other armor.

combat_armor.definition: Military-grade protection. Provides 5+ armor save. Standard issue for professional soldiers.

deflector_field.definition: Advanced energy barrier. Provides 4+ armor save. Rare and expensive.

kerin.definition: Fierce warrior species. +1 to brawling rolls. Natural aggression makes them excellent melee combatants.

soulless.definition: Synthetic beings. Cannot use implants. Immune to morale effects. Cannot carry Bulky weapons.

swift.definition: Agile species. +1 Speed. -1 to be hit by ranged attacks due to quick movement.

engineer.definition: Technically gifted species. +1 Savvy. Can attempt repairs and technical tasks at +1.

hulker.definition: Massive brute species. +2 Toughness. +1 damage in brawling. Cannot fit in standard vehicles.

precursor.definition: Ancient species. +1 to Luck. May reroll one die per battle without spending Luck.

story_points.definition: Narrative currency earned through gameplay. Spent to unlock story events, recruit special characters, or influence outcomes.

upkeep.definition: Credits paid each campaign turn to maintain the crew. Includes food, repairs, and ship maintenance. Unpaid upkeep causes morale problems.

patron.definition: Employers who offer jobs. Patrons provide missions with credits and reputation rewards. Building patron relationships unlocks better jobs.

rival.definition: Hostile faction tracking the crew. Rivals may attack during travel or interfere with missions. Can be removed through combat or negotiation.

quest_rumor.definition: Lead to special story missions. Following rumors reveals quest locations. Completing quests provides unique rewards.

opportunity_mission.definition: Random jobs found while exploring. Lower pay but readily available. Good for crews low on credits.

patron_mission.definition: Jobs assigned by established contacts. Better rewards and reputation gains. May have specific requirements.

quest_mission.definition: Story-driven objectives revealed through Quest Rumors. Unique challenges with narrative significance and special rewards.

rival_attack.definition: Hostile encounter initiated by rivals. Must be resolved before continuing campaign activities. Can occur during travel.

seize_initiative.definition: Deployment condition. Player may attempt to act before normal turn order. Roll Savvy test to seize.

ambush.definition: Deployment condition. Enemy has prepared positions. Player deploys first, enemy gets free actions before battle starts.

delayed_arrival.definition: Deployment condition. Some crew members arrive late. Roll each turn for delayed characters to enter.

implant.definition: Cybernetic enhancement installed in a character. Maximum 2 implants per character. Soulless cannot use implants.

consumable.definition: Single-use item. Expended when used. Includes grenades, stim-packs, and similar items.

credits.definition: Universal currency. Used for purchases, repairs, upkeep, and hiring. Earned through missions and loot.

loot.definition: Items found after battle. Roll on loot tables based on enemy type defeated. Can be equipment, credits, or special items.

casualty.definition: Character removed from battle due to damage exceeding Toughness. Roll on post-battle table to determine fate.

morale.definition: Willingness to continue fighting. When half the force is casualties, test morale. Failure causes retreat.

activation.definition: When a character takes their turn. Characters activate in Reactions order. Each activation allows movement and action.

line_of_sight.definition: Clear path from shooter to target. If any part of target is visible, can be targeted. Blocked by impassable terrain.

range.definition: Maximum distance a weapon can shoot effectively. Beyond range, cannot target. Measured in inches.

damage.definition: Weapon's wounding power. Added to hit roll when determining if target becomes casualty. Higher damage defeats tougher targets.

armor_save.definition: Roll to negate damage. If roll meets or exceeds armor value, damage is ignored. Made after successful hit.
